questions.question: The play ‘Neel Darpan’ is associated with which among the following revolts?
questions.explanation: Neel Darpan was a Bengali play written by Dinabandhu Mitra in 1858–1859. The play was essential to Nilbidraha, or Indigo revolt of February–March 1859 in Bengal, when farmers refused to sow indigo in their fields as a protest against exploitative farming under the British Raj.

questions.question: Which among the following tribal leaders was regarded as an incarnation of God and father of the world ?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda was an Indian tribal freedom fighter and a folk hero, who belonged to the Munda tribe, and was behind the best known Millenarian movement of India that rose in the tribal belt of modern day Bihar, and Jharkhand .Birsa and his disciples set themselves the task of serving the famine-stricken and sick people. He became a legend during his lifetime and was called “Dharati Aba” (father of the earth) and was worshipped as “Birsa Bhagwan”.

questions.question: In which among the following provinces of India, the first signs of unrest during the mutiny of 1857 appeared?
questions.explanation: The first signs of unrest during the mutiny of 1857 was evident in Bengal, Bihar and Odisha. In 1824 it was started with Barrackpore revolt. The actual revolt started on 10th May 1857 in Meerut.

questions.question: Who among the following were the most vocal opponents of Ilbert Bill?

options.label: B
options.text: Advocates and legal practitioners of Kolkata

options.label: C
options.text: British tea and indigo plantation owners in Bengal

answer.label: C
answer.text: British tea and indigo plantation owners in Bengal

questions.explanation: Sir Coutney Pergine Ilbert, the law member of Viceroy's council prepared a bill in 1883 during the viceroy ship of Lord Ripon which is popularly known as Ilbert Bill. It suggested to allow the senior Indian magistrates to preside over cases involving British personnel in India. The British tea and indigo plantations owners in Bengal tremendously opposed the bill.

questions.question: Neel-Darpan’ by Din Bandhu Mitra portrays the plight of :
questions.explanation: Neel Darpan (Bengali play) was written by Dinabandhu Mitra in 1858–1859 and published in Dhaka in 1860. The main context of the play is on the event of Indigo Revolt in Bengal. The play was translated to English by Michael Madhusudan Dutta and published by Reverend James Long.
